label: U.S. Passport — Name Update
description: Update your name on your U.S. passport. A passport serves as both a travel document and a widely accepted form of federal photo ID. The sex designation field is tracked separately — see U.S. Passport — Sex Designation.

process.summary: Submit a passport application (DS-11 for a new passport or first-time applicants; DS-82 if your current passport is less than 15 years old and issued after age 16) with your legal name change documentation and current photos. Applications are submitted at a passport acceptance facility or, for DS-82, by mail.

steps.description: Determine which form applies to you. Use DS-82 if your current passport was issued when you were 16 or older and is less than 15 years old. Use DS-11 for all other situations, including if your name differs significantly from the name on your current passport.
Gather required documents: your legal name change documentation (court order), your current or most recent passport, proof of U.S. citizenship if applying with DS-11, and two recent passport photos.
steps.note: Photos must be 2x2 inches, taken within the last 6 months, against a plain white or off-white background.

steps.description: Complete your application form. Forms are available at travel.state.gov and at many post offices and passport acceptance facilities.

For DS-11 applications: submit in person at a passport acceptance facility. For DS-82 applications: mail your completed application with original documents and payment. Facilities include many post offices and county clerks.
steps.note: Original documents are returned to you along with your new passport.

steps.description: Pay the application fee. Routine processing takes 6 to 8 weeks; expedited processing (additional fee) takes 2 to 3 weeks. Track your application status at travel.state.gov.

documents_required: Proof of U.S. citizenship (for DS-11: original or certified birth certificate)

access_requirements.travel_note: DS-11 applications require an in-person visit to a passport acceptance facility. DS-82 applications can be submitted by mail.

process.cost: $130 application fee plus $35 execution fee for DS-11 (in-person); $130 by mail for DS-82. Expedited processing adds $60. Photo fee ($15–$20) if obtaining photos at the facility.
process.estimated_time_days: 42 to 56 days routine; 14 to 21 days expedited.

presence_level_content.some_guidance: A passport is accepted everywhere a driver's license is, and by many places that do not accept a state ID. If you travel internationally or want a federal ID with no state residency requirement, it is worth doing alongside or shortly after your driver's license.
presence_level_content.walk_with_me: Some people feel relief holding a passport with their correct name — it is one of the most portable pieces of documentation you carry. Others find the fees and wait time discouraging. Both are valid responses to a process that should be simpler.

sources.label: U.S. Department of State: Update a Passport
